pythonScripts.name: Digital Output Cycle Test
pythonScripts.description: Cycles through 4 digital outputs (tag_72-75), turning each ON for 5 seconds then OFF, repeating forever.
pythonScripts.code:
# Digital Output Cycle Test
# Cycles through tag_72, tag_73, tag_74, tag_75
# Each output: ON 5 sec, OFF 5 sec, then next

DIGITAL_OUTPUTS = ['tag_72', 'tag_73', 'tag_74', 'tag_75']
ON_TIME = 5   # seconds ON
OFF_TIME = 5  # seconds OFF before next

print('Digital Output Cycle Test Started')
print(f'Outputs: {DIGITAL_OUTPUTS}')
print(f'ON: {ON_TIME}s, OFF: {OFF_TIME}s')

# Ensure all outputs are OFF at start
for do in DIGITAL_OUTPUTS:
    outputs.set(do, 0)
print('All outputs OFF')

cycle = 0
try:
    while session.active:
        cycle += 1
        print(f'\n--- Cycle {cycle} ---')
        
        for do in DIGITAL_OUTPUTS:
            if not session.active:
                break
            
            # Turn ON
            outputs.set(do, 1)
            print(f'{do} ON')
            publish('ActiveOutput', DIGITAL_OUTPUTS.index(do) + 1)
            wait_for(ON_TIME)
            
            if not session.active:
                break
            
            # Turn OFF and wait
            outputs.set(do, 0)
            print(f'{do} OFF - waiting {OFF_TIME}s')
            wait_for(OFF_TIME)
        
        publish('CycleCount', cycle)

finally:
    print('\nStopping - all outputs OFF')
    for do in DIGITAL_OUTPUTS:
        outputs.set(do, 0)
